instructions: Strictly base all content on the provided inputs and knowledge base.
Do not invent or hallucinate facts, figures, or dates.
If information is missing, explicitly state that it is not available in the context.
If there is no mention in the context of the targeted population size, assume the project targets the full population of interest for the designated field context.
Ensure tone, style, and formatting match a professional humanitarian concept note.
When referencing donor, use donor name and never donor_id
When referencing outcomes, use outcome name and never outcome_id
When referencing, field context, use field_context name and never field context_id
China Aid/GDF templates must be filled using the donor’s official format; generated text must be pasted into the official template.
China Aid proposals must be translated into Chinese before submission; English-only submissions are not accepted.
All proposals must be vetted by UNHCR Beijing before contacting Chinese embassies.
Use only SDG numbers and sub-goal numbers (no full names). Limit to 1–2 major SDGs and ≤3 sub-goals; for CRI/NFI, SDG 1 and sub-goal 1.5 are usually appropriate.
Cite publicly available sources (UNHCR/OCHA/WB/OECD) for socio-economic data and humanitarian needs; if not available, state not available.

sections.instructions: Briefly summarize the crisis/emergency context, humanitarian needs, target population, and key proposed activities.
Justify the project within the field context using recent (≤6 months) public sources (UNHCR, OCHA, WB, OECD). Do not include activities here.
Describe the main proposed interventions, expected outputs, and indicators; align with no more than 1–2 SDGs (e.g., SDG 1, sub-goal 1.5 for CRI/NFI).
Describe coordination with government (e.g., Commission for Refugees) and other humanitarian actors, and roles of partners.
Provide a high-level budget breakdown by major category. Do not reference explicit budget buffers.

columns.name: Category
columns.instructions: Major budget category (e.g., Staffing, Supplies, Logistics).

rows.instructions: Estimate indirect/support costs (e.g., 6.5% ISC as applicable).

sections.instructions: Provide a brief introduction of the Applicant: UNHCR Representation in [Country], highlighting experience and technical capacity relevant to the project.
Introduce project partner(s) (e.g., government and NGOs), with mandates and roles relevant to this CN.
Describe visibility activities including use of the China Aid logo per Administrative Measures, signage at distributions, and photo documentation.
Describe the management structure of Applicant, Partner(s), and field-level roles in text (no image needed).
